portfolio_rationale: GOOGL and AAPL lead with judge confidence of 0.85 each, clean fundamental profiles, and positive technical momentum across multiple timeframes; GOOGL edges ahead due to zero liquidity risk flags and a very low debt-to-equity ratio. LLY has exceptional fundamentals (85% revenue growth, 83% gross margin) but is penalized by bearish news/technical signals and litigation risk. BRK.B has negative revenue growth and weak technicals, making it a lower-conviction hold. AMZN is technically broken across all momentum indicators with a bearish technical signal at 0.9 confidence, reducing conviction despite strong fundamentals. XOM receives zero weight as the judge returned a neutral signal.

GOOGL.reason: Highest-quality bull case with 0.85 judge confidence, no liquidity or leverage risk, strong ROE/ROA, positive technical momentum, and the bear rebuttal was weak (debt-to-equity of 0.087 is actually very low, not high).

AAPL.reason: Strong fundamentals across all profitability metrics, positive technical momentum with price above both 20d and 50d MAs, and 0.85 judge confidence; minor liquidity concern is manageable given massive free cash flow.

LLY.reason: Exceptional fundamental profile with 85% revenue growth and 83% gross margin driving 0.85 judge confidence, but bearish news sentiment, litigation risk, and price below 20d MA warrant a reduced position.

BRK.B.reason: Solid profitability metrics and positive macro support a 0.70 judge confidence bullish signal, but negative revenue growth and weak technicals across all momentum indicators limit conviction.

AMZN.reason: Strong fundamentals are overshadowed by a technically bearish setup with all six momentum indicators negative and a 0.90-confidence bearish technical signal, warranting only a small position on fundamental value.

XOM.reason: Judge returned a neutral signal with 0.50 confidence; bull and bear cases were deemed too close to justify a directional trade.
